does: This paper proposes a framework for training embodied world models that enables broader exploration beyond conservative near-distribution rollouts by addressing reward hacking through verification. The core contribution is Reward as an Agent, an agentic reward framework that actively evaluates generated behaviors to provide robust reward signals under distribution shifts. The method combines this with Dynamic-Aware Rollout Diversification to expand exploration while maintaining reliability. The approach is evaluated in embodied world model settings where physical plausibility and task completion provide rigorous testbeds, demonstrating improved dynamic discovery and behavioral diversity compared to existing conservative RL methods for world model refinement.
connects: This work builds on the growing use of reinforcement learning for improving world models, which has emerged as a complementary approach to supervised pretraining for learning environmental dynamics. It responds directly to limitations in existing methods that restrict rollouts to stay near the training distribution to avoid distribution shift problems. The paper engages with classical concerns about reward hacking and specification gaming that have motivated conservative exploration strategies, while drawing on recent research in robust reward design and agentic approaches to learning. It extends prior work on verification and validation in learned models, particularly in embodied AI and physical simulation, where ensuring physical plausibility under exploration has been a persistent challenge.
enables: By decoupling the twin problems of exploration and verification through the Reward as an Agent framework, this work opens pathways for more ambitious RL-based world model training that can discover richer dynamics without sacrificing safety. The approach could enable world models that generalize more effectively to out-of-distribution scenarios by exposing them to diverse behaviors during training while maintaining principled evaluation. Future work might extend agentic reward verification to other domains beyond embodied settings, develop more sophisticated verification strategies that scale to high-dimensional behavior spaces, or combine this approach with other robustness techniques to further mitigate specification gaming in reward learning.
